description: Get an event by ID: msgraph-calendar-get-event
examples:
Command Example
!msgraph-calendar-list-calendars user=someemail@domain.com
Context Example
{
"MSGraphCalendar.Calendar": [
{
"CanEdit": true,
"CanShare": true,

!msgraph-calendar-list-calendars user=someemail@domain.com
Context Example
{
"MSGraphCalendar.Calendar": [
{
"CanEdit": true,
"CanShare": true,
"CanViewPrivateItems": true,
name: msgraph-calendar-list-calendars

outputs.description: The name of the calendar. MSGraphCalendar.Calendar.CanShare Whether the user has permission to share the calendar. Only the user who created the calendar can share.
outputs.name: MSGraphCalendar.Calendar.Name

outputs.description: Whether the user can write to the calendar (this is true for the user who created the calendar and for a user who has been granted access to a shared calendar). MSGraphCalendar.Calendar.ChangeKey Identifies the version of the calendar object. Every time the calendar is changed, the changeKey changes as well. This allows the exchange to apply changes to the correct version of the object. Read-only.
outputs.name: MSGraphCalendar.Calendar.CanEdit

outputs.description: The user who created or added the calendar. MSGraphCalendar.Calendar.ID The unique ID of the calendar. Read-only.
outputs.name: MSGraphCalendar.Calendar.Owner

outputs.description: Whether the user can read calendar items that have been marked private.
outputs.name: MSGraphCalendar.Calendar.CanViewPrivateItems
